channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks, Trump says
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire by 3 weeks. Live updates: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks, Trump says AP News.

item.title: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
item.description: The EU approved a 90bn euro loan to Ukraine and new Russia sanctions. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

item.title: Trading Day: Geopolitical reality check
item.description: No information is available on this topic. Trading Day: Geopolitical reality check.

item.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
item.description: Afghan refugees who helped US forces may be sent to Congo. Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U. S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or returning home to live under the Taliban.

item.title: Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks
item.description: Trump will meet Lebanon and Israel envoys during peace talks after a 10-day truce was announced. The United States announced a 10-day truce shortly after the first meeting. Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks.

item.title: Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran
item.description: Iran's military establishment took control after Mojtaba was injured. The balance of power appears firmly tilted toward the military establishment. Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran.

item.title: How Trump’s Iran war is driving military dissent
item.description: Dissent is rising inside the US military over the US-Israel war on Iran with protests and quiet resistance. From protests to quiet resistance, dissent is rising inside the United States military over the US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Microsoft Offers Voluntary Retirement To 8,750 Employees In US
item.description: Microsoft offered voluntary retirement to 8,750 US employees, allowing them to leave the company with severance packages. The move is part of Microsoft's efforts to trim expenses. Big tech companies have been looking for ways to trim their expenses.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: The E. U. approved a $106 billion loan for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending. This reflects the E. U.'s view that peace in Ukraine is unlikely in the near future. Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

item.title: Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine
item.description: Prince Harry made a surprise visit to Ukraine, meeting with officials and civilians. His visit aimed to show support for Ukraine's people during the ongoing conflict. Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos.

item.title: Before Iran War, China Amassed World's Largest Oil Stockpile
item.description: China amassed the world's largest oil stockpile before the Iran war, a move likely aimed at securing its energy supply. China is the world's largest crude oil importer, making this stockpile crucial to its economy.

item.title: D4vd's iPhone Contained 'Significant' Amount Of Child Pornography, Prosecutors Claim
item.description: Prosecutors claim D4vd's iPhone contained a significant amount of child pornography, discovered during an investigation into his iCloud storage. The exact nature of the images is not specified. D4vd's iCloud storage contains child pornography found by prosecutors during investigation.

item.title: Russia reports toxic rain, orders locals to stay home after Ukrainian oil strike
item.description: Russia reported toxic rain and ordered locals to stay home after a Ukrainian oil strike in Tuapse on April 20. The strike triggered a huge blaze and sent thick black smoke into the sky over the coastal city.

item.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
item.description: Forty-three people were indicted on charges including murder and extortion in a California crackdown on the Mexican mafia.

item.title: Trump shares right-wing anchor’s rant against Indians, MEA terms it ‘inappropriate’
item.description: The Indian government termed Donald Trump's shared rant against Indians as inappropriate after opposition criticism. The MEA response came after Opposition members and former diplomats criticised the government for not offering a stronger response to the U. S.

item.title: Pentagon says top Navy official to leave office ‘immediately’
item.description: The Navy's top civilian official is leaving office immediately, the Pentagon said, without giving a reason, as the Navy blockades Iranian ports. No reason was given for the unexpected departure of the Navy’s top civilian official, coming as the sea service has imposed a blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: Teenager becomes first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan's Karachi
item.description: A teenager in Karachi became the first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan. Concern among health officials in Karachi with the festival of Id al-Azha due next month. Teenager becomes first Congo virus fatality in Pakistan's Karachi.

item.title: Above 92% turnout in Bengal, 85% in Tamil Nadu amid poll violence | Above the Fold | 23.04.2026
item.description: Bengal saw 92% voter turnout and Tamil Nadu 85% amid poll violence. In today’s episode, we look at the key election trends in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, tracking major shifts ahead of polling.

item.title: Journalist Detained in Kuwait After Posting About Iran War Is Acquitted
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a Kuwaiti-American journalist, was acquitted after 52 days in detention in Kuwait. Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, a Kuwaiti-American journalist, is expected to be released soon, after 52 days in detention, a lawyer for two of his family members said.

item.title: Huge chunk of glacier blocks Everest route in peak climbing season
item.description: A huge block of ice is blocking the Everest route, preventing Sherpas from preparing it for climbers. Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world's highest peak because a huge block of ice is in the way.
